subject: DevOps
title: Object Storage: S3 and Beyond
description: A short lesson to help you apply Object Storage: S3 and Beyond.

hook.text: S3 stores trillions of objects. Netflix streams from it. It's the backbone of cloud storage.

buildup.text: Object storage holds files, images, backups, and logs as objects with metadata. No file hierarchy.

discovery.text: S3 offers eleven nines of durability. Data replicates across facilities automatically.

twist.text: Storage tiers save money. Glacier and archive tiers cost less but have retrieval delays.

climax.text: Lifecycle policies automate tiering. Move to cheaper storage at 30 days. Delete at a year.

punchline.text: Object storage: durable, scalable, cheap. Store everything.

quiz.question: What does S3's eleven nines of durability mean?
options: 99.999999999% — virtually zero chance of data loss
The data can be accessed in 11 milliseconds
Up to 11 copies of each file are stored
